name: Germany
description: The German flag features three equal horizontal bands of black, red, and gold. The colors date to the early 19th century and were used by the Frankfurt Parliament in 1848.

name: France
description: The French tricolor features three equal vertical bands of blue, white, and red. Adopted during the French Revolution in 1794, the colors represent liberty, equality, and fraternity.

name: Italy
description: The Italian tricolor consists of three equal vertical bands of green, white, and red. Inspired by the French flag, it was first adopted in 1797 and became the national flag in 1948.

name: Belgium
description: The Belgian flag features three equal vertical bands of black, yellow, and red. The colors derive from the arms of the Duchy of Brabant, used since the Belgian Revolution of 1830.

name: Netherlands
description: The Dutch flag has three equal horizontal bands of red, white, and blue. One of the oldest tricolor flags in the world, it dates to the 16th-century Dutch Revolt.

name: Austria
description: The Austrian flag consists of three equal horizontal bands of red, white, and red. According to legend it originates from the Battle of Ptolemais in 1191 and is one of the oldest national flags in continuous use.

name: Poland
description: The Polish flag has two equal horizontal bands of white over red. The colors come from the coat of arms of Poland — a white eagle on a red shield.

name: Ukraine
description: The Ukrainian flag features two equal horizontal bands of azure blue over golden yellow, symbolizing blue skies above golden wheat fields.

name: Hungary
description: The Hungarian flag has three equal horizontal bands of red, white, and green. The colors appear in Hungarian culture since the 13th century and were formally adopted in 1848.

name: Estonia
description: The Estonian flag has three equal horizontal bands of blue, black, and white — representing the sky, the dark past, and the bright future of Estonia. First adopted in 1884.

name: Latvia
description: The Latvian flag features two dark red stripes with a narrower white stripe in between, in a 2:1:2 ratio. One of the oldest national flag designs, dating to 1279.

name: Lithuania
description: The Lithuanian flag has three equal horizontal bands of yellow, green, and red. The colors symbolize ripening wheat, forests and hope, and the blood of fallen heroes.

name: Bulgaria
description: The Bulgarian flag has three equal horizontal bands of white, green, and red. White symbolizes peace, green represents the fertile land, and red stands for the valor of its defenders.

name: Armenia
description: The Armenian flag has three equal horizontal bands of red, blue, and orange. Red symbolizes the Armenian Highlands, blue the sky, and orange the creative talent and hard-working nature of the Armenian people.

name: Ireland
description: The Irish tricolor has three equal vertical bands of green, white, and orange. Green represents Irish Catholics, orange represents Irish Protestants, and white symbolizes peace between them.

name: Russia
description: The Russian flag features three equal horizontal bands of white, blue, and red. The tricolor was introduced by Peter the Great in the 17th century, inspired by the Dutch flag.

name: Colombia
description: The Colombian flag features three horizontal bands: a wide yellow band on top occupying half the flag, and equal blue and red bands below. The colors symbolize gold, the sky, and the blood of Colombian patriots.

name: Guinea
description: The Guinean flag has three equal vertical bands of red, yellow, and green — the Pan-African colors — representing sacrifice and revolution, wealth and the sun, and the forests.

name: Mali
description: The Malian flag features three equal vertical bands of green, yellow, and red. The Pan-African colors symbolize Islam and solidarity, natural wealth, and the blood of those who died defending the nation.

name: Sierra Leone
description: The Sierra Leonean flag has three equal horizontal bands of green, white, and blue. Green represents the natural resources, white stands for unity and justice, and blue symbolizes hope for peace.

name: Cameroon
description: The Cameroonian flag has three equal vertical bands of green, red, and yellow. Green represents the southern forests, red symbolizes independence and unity, and yellow represents the savannahs of the north.

name: Indonesia
description: The Indonesian flag, known as Sang Merah Putih (the Red and White), has two equal horizontal bands of red and white. The colors appear in the Majapahit Empire and represent courage and purity.

name: Chad
description: The Chadian flag has three equal vertical bands of blue, yellow, and red. Blue represents the sky and hope, yellow symbolizes the sun and desert, and red stands for progress and the blood of martyrs.

name: Norway
description: Red with a blue cross outlined in white. The cross — shifted toward the hoist — is the traditional Christian symbol of Scandinavia. Colors represent the country's past unions with Denmark and Sweden. Adopted 1821.

name: Sweden
description: Blue with a yellow Nordic cross shifted toward the hoist. The gold cross on blue derives from the Swedish coat of arms. One of the oldest national flag designs, in continuous use since the 16th century.

name: Denmark
description: Red with a white Nordic cross shifted toward the hoist. Known as the Dannebrog, it is one of the oldest national flags in the world, reportedly used since the 13th century.

name: Finland
description: White with a blue Nordic cross shifted toward the hoist. The blue cross represents the lakes and sky of Finland; white represents snow. Adopted as the national flag in 1918 after independence from Russia.

name: Iceland
description: Blue with a white-outlined red Nordic cross shifted toward the hoist. Blue and white represent the sky, ice, and snow; red represents the volcanic fire of the island. Adopted 1944.

name: Switzerland
description: Red square with a white centered Greek cross whose arms are one-sixth longer than wide. One of only two square national flags. The white cross on red has represented the Swiss Confederation since the 14th century.

name: Japan
description: White with a centered red disc representing the sun — the Hinomaru, or 'circle of the sun'. Japan is historically called the 'Land of the Rising Sun'. The current proportions were standardized in 1999.

name: Czech Republic
description: Two equal horizontal bands of white over red with a blue isosceles triangle on the hoist side. Blue was added when Czechoslovakia was formed in 1920 to distinguish it from the Polish and Austrian flags.

name: Philippines
description: Two equal horizontal bands of blue over red with a white equilateral triangle on the hoist. The blue and red bands are reversed during wartime. Adopted 1898 during the Philippine Revolution.

name: Jamaica
description: A diagonal gold cross (saltire) divides the flag into four triangles: green at top and bottom, black at left and right. Green symbolizes the land, gold represents natural wealth and sunlight, black stands for hardships overcome. Adopted 1962.
